_docs.purpose: Single source of truth for the CLI-visible knob surface: every config key, dotted config path, CLI flag, embedder (engine request) field, and external-tool flag that a zzop message is allowed to name. Consumed by crates/config (the Rust config front-end's unknown-key drift warnings) AND by crates/engine/tests/rule_contracts/'s reference-validation meta-test (CHECK A / CHECK B), so both sides of the contract read the exact same data ‚Äî a config key or flag can never drift between what the config front-end accepts and what the engine's own rule/warning messages are allowed to recommend.
_docs.configKeys: The fixed-shape config object's known keys, by scope (originally the removed JS mapper's KNOWN_KEYS constant; now the Rust config front-end's). See crates/config's own doc for the 'warn on drift, never reject' contract this backs. Path resolution bases: `trees[].root` is resolved to absolute against a caller-supplied `base_dir` by the config front-end (crates/config), see crates/config/src/mapper/paths.rs's doc comment; `overlays` entries (both top-level and `trees[].overlays`) are instead resolved by both dialects against the OWNING TREE's own root, never `base_dir`. For the zzop-mcp server specifically (verified against crates/host/src/tools.rs + crates/summary/src/paths.rs + crates/config/src/lib.rs): `base_dir` is NOT a fixed value ‚Äî it is either the tool call's own (already-absolutized) tree root (`analyze_repo`, and `cross_repo`'s config-free `paths` mode), or the directory containing the (already-absolutized) `configPath` (`cross_repo`'s config-first mode). The absolutizing step itself is the actionable part: a RELATIVE `path`/`paths`/`configPath` tool argument is resolved by the MCP HOST PROCESS against ITS OWN current working directory (`std::path::absolute`, i.e. wherever the client happened to launch/configure the `zzop-mcp mcp` process) ‚Äî a directory the calling MCP client cannot see or rely on. Consequently: always pass ABSOLUTE paths for the `path`/`paths`/`configPath` tool arguments in MCP usage, and prefer an absolute `trees[].root` when authoring a `zzop.config.jsonc` consumed via `configPath`, so no part of the join depends on the server process's unseen cwd. `packs.extraDirs` is the config-file dialect for adding a DSL rule-pack directory ‚Äî do not write `packsDir` in a `zzop.config.jsonc` (that's the embedder request field a host application sets directly; see `embedderFieldShapes.packsDir` for its own back-reference).
_docs.rulesValues: A `rules.<ruleId>` entry (see crates/config/src/mapper/options.rs ‚Äî the Rust config front end accepts EXACTLY two shapes and rejects everything else) is either of exactly two shapes: (1) a severity/disable STRING token, or (2) an OBJECT `{ severity, exclude }` (the `ruleObject` shape above) whose `severity` key (optional) takes the same string token and whose `exclude` key (optional) is an array of path substrings/globs. The string form maps through `SEVERITY_ALIASES`: `critical`/`error`/`err`/`high` -> critical, `warning`/`warn`/`medium` -> warning, `info`/`information`/`note`/`low` -> info, and `off`/`none`/`disable`/`disabled` disables the rule entirely ‚Äî `"off"` (or any of its aliases) is how a rule is disabled via config, as opposed to the embedder dialect's `disabled_rules`/`disabledRules` list. ANY other value shape ‚Äî a bare boolean (`false` included ‚Äî there is no boolean dialect; writing `"ruleId": false` to disable a rule is invalid, use `"off"`), a number, an array, `null`, or an object without `severity`/`exclude` ‚Äî is rejected with a load-time `ConfigError` naming the rule id (`rules.<ruleId> must be a severity string (e.g. "warn"/"off") or an object ({ "severity": ..., "exclude": [...] }).`), never silently ignored and never treated as disabling the rule.
_docs.configPaths: Every valid DOTTED spelling of a nested config key (derived from configKeys), in the form an author would actually write it in a message: `<parent>.<child>` for a fixed-shape nested object (e.g. `git.since`), `<parent>[].<child>` for a per-entry field of an array-of-objects (e.g. `trees[].root`). Deliberately excludes `rules.<id>.severity`/`rules.<id>.exclude`: `<id>` is an open-keyed rule id, not enumerable here (a message naming a specific rule id under `rules` is checked as a JSON-snippet shape instead, see the rule_contracts meta-test's CHECK B doc).
_docs.cliFlags: The flags the `zzop` binary recognizes (packages/cli-bin/src/main.rs): `--config` (the config-first modifier for `cross`/`endpoint`), `--version`, and `--help`/`-h`. The binary otherwise dispatches on SUBCOMMANDS (`analyze`, `cross`, `endpoint`, `analyze-envelope`, `validate-envelope`, `validate-rule-pack`, `contract`, `version`, `help` ‚Äî see main.rs's USAGE constant), not flags. This list is the whitelist CHECK A validates every `--flag`-shaped token in shipped messages against (union with `externalToolFlags`).
_docs.embedderFields: engine request-surface field names (crates/facade/src/lib.rs's AnalyzeRequest) that legitimately appear in a zzop message as 'the embedder dialect' ‚Äî i.e. the field name a HOST APPLICATION embedding the engine (`zzop-facade`) directly (not through a config file) would set. Every native rule's disable-hint already says this explicitly: 'Disable via config `rules: {...}` (embedders: `disabled_rules`)'. Kept to exactly the fields a message in the scanned dirs actually references today (see the rule_contracts meta-test's CHECK B doc comment for the verification) ‚Äî extend only when a new message legitimately needs to name another embedder field, not preemptively.
_docs.embedderFieldShapes: One-line JSON shape per embedderFields entry, so an embedder field named in a zzop message is discoverable from this file alone (a consumer holding only the binary + this resource would otherwise see a bare name with no shape). Documentation only ‚Äî no code path consumes this section (lib/mapper.js reads configKeys; the rule_contracts meta-test reads the flat lists); keep its key set mirroring embedderFields exactly.
_docs.externalToolFlags: Flags of OTHER tools (never zzop's own) that a zzop message legitimately mentions when explaining an OUTSIDE cause for a symptom. Today that's exactly git's own `--depth` (a shallow clone, e.g. `git clone --depth=1`) and `--unshallow` (`git fetch --unshallow`, the fix) ‚Äî both from crates/metrics/src/diagnostics.rs's thin-git-history warning. Kept minimal: enumerated by scanning the real tree, not pre-populated with every flag a tool happens to have.
_docs.allowlistedTokens: Literal tokens that are not config paths/keys at all but legitimately sit inside CHECK B's +/-120-char 'near the word config' window anyway ‚Äî a filename convention (`zzop.config.jsonc`, the CLI's own config file, referenced as plain prose text today but allowlisted preemptively so a future backtick-quoted mention doesn't spuriously fail) or an unrelated identifier/header name that happens to share a sentence with a config-disable hint. Each entry's source is documented in the rule_contracts meta-test's CHECK B doc comment, not just here. `ir.io` ‚Äî packages/mcp/src/tools/definitions.rs's analyze_repo/cross_repo descriptions name it as the raw-io OUTPUT field their own shaped MCP reply omits (the same co-location pattern as `crossLayer.unresolvedConsumes`: an output field, not an input config knob, mentioned near a config-honoring clause in the same sentence).
_docs.mcpToolTokens: The zzop-mcp host's tool-surface vocabulary: every tools/call ARGUMENT name (packages/mcp/src/tools/definitions.rs inputSchema properties, enumerated by reading that file) plus the reply fields its self-description text backticks (`config`, `configWarnings`, `ruleOverridesApplied`, `bucketKeySites`, `ir`). A DIFFERENT dialect from config-file keys ‚Äî vouched as its own section (not allowlist noise) since crates/host/src joined the reference-validation scan set (2026-07-17; the file itself moved to packages/mcp/src in the 2026-07-23 product-layer split, still inside the scanned set), so a tool description naming a nonexistent argument fails CHECK B the same way a bad config key does. `ir` was added when analyze_repo/cross_repo's descriptions started naming the raw engine field their shaped MCP reply deliberately omits (see this file's `allowlistedTokens` doc for the paired `ir.io` entry ‚Äî the dotted form is checked as an allowlisted token, not through this list, since CHECK B's dotted-token branch does not consult mcpToolTokens). `packsLoaded` was added when `validate_rule_pack`'s description started pointing authors at it (2026-07-17): the tool validates one pack's shape in isolation, so a pack `id` collision with a bundled/other pack ‚Äî which replaces the loser WHOLE ‚Äî is invisible to it and only surfaces at load time via the engine's `packsLoaded` report (plus its shadow warning), which the description now names by backtick. `architecture` and `gitWindow` were added (2026-07-17, git-signal disclosure-asymmetry fix batch) when analyze_repo's description started naming its own new reply field (`architecture`: a compact, capped `{pain, topRecommendation, criticalTop}` summary of the facade's `health`/`recommendations`/`critical` output, present only when git signals ran) and the forwarded engine echo field `gitWindow` ({recentDays, since}).
_docs.treeFields: Per-field type/requiredness for `configKeys.tree` (one entry of a `trees[]` array), read from crates/config/src/mapper.rs + crates/config/src/mapper/validation.rs (the config front end's own fail-fast gates): `root` ‚Äî string, REQUIRED, non-empty; a path resolved against `base_dir` (see the path-resolution _docs paragraph below). `sourceId` ‚Äî string, OPTIONAL; when omitted, defaults to the tree's OWN raw `root` string exactly as written (e.g. a tree with `root: "./api"` and no `sourceId` gets `sourceId: "./api"` ‚Äî NOT the resolved absolute path and NOT just the root's basename). That is the CONFIG-file dialect default (this `trees[]`/`roots` mapping); a config-less/bare-path analyze request (no config file, no `trees[]` in play at all ‚Äî e.g. an embedder calling `zzop-facade`'s analyze directly with a bare `root`) instead defaults an empty/missing `sourceId` to the analyzed root DIRECTORY's own basename, applied at the facade chokepoint every host funnels through (`apply_source_id_default`, `crates/facade/src/analyze.rs`, D14 2026-07-17) ‚Äî lossy `to_str` fallback: the given root string verbatim if the basename can't be decoded (e.g. a bare drive root). `overlays` ‚Äî string[], OPTIONAL; each entry is a path to a Mode B adapter envelope JSON file, resolved against THIS TREE'S OWN root (never `base_dir`, never the config file's directory ‚Äî see `configKeys`'s own overlay-resolution note); an unreadable or non-JSON overlay is skipped with a warning, never a hard error. `mountedAt` ‚Äî string, OPTIONAL; a whole-tree gateway/ingress prefix shorthand, validated to start with `/`, contain no `://` scheme, no `{}` placeholder, and no whitespace (a violation is a load-time `ConfigError`, not a warning). `mounts` ‚Äî array of `{ dir, at }` objects, OPTIONAL; `dir` must be tree-relative (must NOT start with `/`) and use forward slashes only (no backslash); `at` follows the same validation as `mountedAt`. `hosts` ‚Äî string[], OPTIONAL; each entry a BARE host (no `://` scheme, no `/` path separator, no whitespace) ‚Äî a full URL is rejected. `routes` ‚Äî array of `{ key, role? }` objects, OPTIONAL; the lightweight route-fact injection channel for a route zzop could not resolve from source (a non-literal path, a dynamic verb, a computed URL): `key` is a required non-empty `"METHOD PATH"` string (e.g. `"GET /api/users"`, normalized through the same `http_interface_key` the extractors use so casing/trailing-slash don't matter), `role` is optional and exactly `"provide"` (default ‚Äî the route is SERVED here) or `"consume"` (the route is CALLED from here). The facade expands the whole array into ONE synthetic adapter overlay of http provides/consumes (`crates/facade/src/config.rs`'s `routes_overlay`), so it composes through the identical cross-layer join path as a hand-authored `overlays` envelope; a `key` that is not a METHOD+PATH pair is soft-skipped with a warning at that layer (not a load-time `ConfigError`). Separately, `trees` itself (the array's own top-level key, sibling to `roots`) accepts the literal string `"auto"` instead of an array: this expands to one tree per workspace package (`sourceId` = each package's name), detected from `pnpm-workspace.yaml` or `package.json`'s `workspaces` field (crates/config/src/workspaces.rs) ‚Äî a monorepo shortcut that turns the cross-layer join on with no hand-authored `trees[]` array. `roots` vs `trees: "auto"`: `trees: "auto"` always scans the CONFIG FILE'S OWN directory for workspace members; `roots` never steers auto mode (it has zero effect once `trees: "auto"` is in play). A config carrying BOTH is a conflict, not a silent merge ‚Äî auto wins, `roots` is dropped, and the config gets one `configWarnings` entry naming that `roots` was ignored (`crates/config/src/workspaces.rs`'s `expand_auto_trees`).

embedderFieldShapes.adapterOverlays: NormalizedEnvelope[] ‚Äî mode-B io overlays (partial envelopes, typically io + fragment channels for a few files) merged on top of native analysis for the tree; an invalid overlay is skipped with a warning
embedderFieldShapes.disabled_rules: string[] of rule ids ‚Äî the snake_case spelling native-rule disable-hints use for the same list as disabledRules
embedderFieldShapes.disabledRules: string[] of rule ids ‚Äî those rules never run/report
embedderFieldShapes.packsDir: string | string[] ‚Äî DSL rule-pack directory path(s), loaded in order (a later same-id pack replaces the earlier one whole); explicit null opts out of every DSL pack, bundled included. Config-file dialect: `packs.extraDirs` ‚Äî `packsDir` is the embedder request field, not a valid zzop.config.jsonc key.
embedderFieldShapes.suppressions: { rule, path?, glob? }[] ‚Äî drops findings for one rule (path = substring match, glob = full-path anchored; glob wins when both are set)
